records.ety: [OE. mede, AS. meodo; akin to D. mede, G. met, meth, OHG. metu, mitu, Icel. mjöðr, Dan. miöd, Sw. mjöd, Russ. med', Lith. midus, W. medd, Gr. me`qy wine, Skr. madhu honey, a sweet drink, as adj., sweet. √270. Cf. Metheglin.]

defs: A fermented drink made of water and honey with malt, yeast, etc.; metheglin; hydromel.
A drink composed of sirup of sarsaparilla or other flavoring extract, and water. It is sometimes charged with carbonic acid gas.

quotes: A mede All full of freshe flowers, white and reede.
To fertile vales and dewy meads My weary, wandering steps he leads.
